title: Exception Handling & Error Rendering
description: Catching exceptions in Laravel Handler, reporting to log/Datadog, rendering HTTP responses or error views. This is a Laravel 5.7 app instrumented with the Datadog PHP tracing SDK.
activity_flow: START: Exception thrown during request lifecycle
Laravel kernel catches exception, invokes Handler::report($exception)
YES → parent::report() logs exception (via configured log driver)
NO  → DDTrace hook fires but marks error.ignored = 1
DECISION: Is this a 'Datadog blocked the request' exception?
YES → LaravelIntegration::Handler::shouldntReport hook overrides return to TRUE (suppress report)
LaravelIntegration::Handler::render hook replaces exception with LaravelIntegrationException()
DECISION: Is exception a 'Datadog blocked the request' exception?
YES → Substitute LaravelIntegrationException, let parent render a clean response
DECISION: Is response status >= 500 and error not ignored?
END: Span closed with error metadata (if applicable)
key_decision_points: shouldReport($exception): determines whether exception is written to logs and flagged as error in Datadog
shouldntReport hook: Datadog integration intercepts to suppress 'blocked request' exceptions from reporting
render hook: Datadog integration substitutes exception object for blocked requests before rendering
Response status code >= 500 + error.ignored flag: determines if rootSpan gets exception attached on send
key_methods: App\Exceptions\Handler::render($request, Exception $exception): Response — delegates to parent::render()
Illuminate\Foundation\Exceptions\Handler::render($request, Throwable $e): Response — intercepted by DDTrace hook
Illuminate\Http\Response::send() — hooked to attach exception to rootSpan when status >= 500
datadog_hooks: hook_method on Illuminate\Contracts\Debug\ExceptionHandler::report (prehook): sets rootSpan->exception and error.ignored flag
install_hook on Illuminate\Foundation\Exceptions\Handler::shouldntReport (posthook): overrides return value to true for 'Datadog blocked the request' exceptions
install_hook on Illuminate\Foundation\Exceptions\Handler::render (prehook): replaces exception arg with LaravelIntegrationException for blocked requests
hook_method on Illuminate\Http\Response::send (prehook): assigns exception to rootSpan if status >= 500 and not ignored
hook_method on Symfony\Component\Console\Application::renderException/renderThrowable: sets rootSpan->exception for CLI exceptions
key_properties: Handler::$dontReport = [] — exception types excluded from reporting
Handler::$dontFlash = ['password', 'password_confirmation'] — inputs never flashed on validation errors
key_relationships: LaravelIntegration hooks into ExceptionHandler::report, ::shouldntReport, ::render via DDTrace instrumentation
LaravelIntegration depends on LaravelIntegrationException (substitution for blocked-request exceptions)
DDTrace root span receives error metadata from both Handler::report hook and Response::send hook
important_notes: This is a Datadog PHP tracing project (dd-trace-php). The app/Exceptions/Handler.php is a thin Laravel 5.7 stub with no custom logic — all exception-to-Datadog wiring is done via DDTrace::hook_method/install_hook inside LaravelIntegration::init(). The 'Datadog blocked the request' path is an AppSec (WAF) blocking flow that suppresses error reporting and returns a neutral response. The 'error.ignored' meta flag on the root span controls whether Datadog counts the exception as an error in APM.
